mission.title: alertmanager: Generally enable reading secrets from files
mission.description: Generally enable reading secrets from files. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current alertmanager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your alertmanager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
helm list -n alertmanager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working alertmanager installation.

steps.title: Review alertmanager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant alertmanager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl get configmap -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=alertmanager
```
This is the same as https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/8551 , just for Alertmanager.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Generally enable reading secrets from files
steps.description:
I found a workaround for Opsgenie, Pagerduty and Pushover:

While coding https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager/pull/2728 I detect a hidden functionality inside Alertmanager.

I saw this functionally for pagerduty and pushover

 * pagerduty.service_key_file
   
```yaml
{{ define "pushover.default.user_key" }}API_KEY{{ end }}
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl get events -n alertmanager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Generally enable reading secrets from files" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I found a workaround for Opsgenie, Pagerduty and Pushover:

While coding https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager/pull/2728 I detect a hidden functionality inside Alertmanager.

I saw this functionally for pagerduty and pushover

 * pagerduty.service_key_file
    https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager/blob/70abcccedba5b111f9215e79f2842066db0ffbec/notify/pagerduty/pagerduty.go#L157
 *

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with alertmanager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
